channel.title: Alex Wilson's writing
channel.description: Alex on engineering, products & everything in-between

item.title: Weeknotes: Only Happy When It Rains — Week 23, 2026
item.description: Lots of small things on the docket this week, which made me think back to Rocks, Pebbles & Sand. I broke down all of my ongoing work and priorities in this way and shared it with my team. Hopefully this will earn me…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Lots of small things on the docket this week, which made me think back to Rocks, Pebbles & Sand. I broke down all of my ongoing work and priorities in this way and shared it with my team. Hopefully this will earn me spinach.
  • One of the pebbles was starting a trial for an internal tool which had been blocked by various approvals for over a month. After waiting over a month, it was very satisfying to see my work, “just work”.
  • This week we had a typhoon, which wasn’t super severe, but it was my first time (I think) actually sitting through one. The air was heavy, and everything was wet.
  • Thursday night a few of us gathered to enjoy souvenirs from Italy and Poland, and this was heaps of fun.
  • Friday night I went to a nomikai and made a fatal mistake, finding myself a couple of megas ahead. I overdid it slightly and had to sit out for a bit, and got home in the wee hours.
  • Met up with some friends on Saturday to go to pride, which was more-or-less the same as last year. Lots of sponsors…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Cure — Week 22, 2026
item.description: Another week, another special assignment. This one particularly interesting as it involves applying AI in a novel way. We do a lot of prep over the week. At the same time, I have started writing objectives for the…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Another week, another special assignment. This one particularly interesting as it involves applying AI in a novel way. We do a lot of prep over the week. At the same time, I have started writing objectives for the next two quarters which is surprisingly hard.
  • Yakiniku on Monday night is very good. This won’t be my last time at GEMs this week.
  • Hey, good news, I no longer have braces! Bad news: I now have a retainer. My dentist is positively chuffed that we got here, almost exactly six months after we started. I'm just glad that it'll be quicker to brush my teeth.
  • The project I’ve been focussed on recently has a nice little nomikai and I have a few equally nice 1:1s. It’s night and day where we are now compared to where we started.
  • I received a gift mid-week: Club Mate!!!!!
  • This week is month-end, which for me now means a decent chunk of paperwork. I’m summoned to a dinner, where I’m a bit negative because I’m in a weird mood for many reasons and trying to convince anyone who’ll…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: The More I See — Week 21, 2026
item.description: Monday I took the day off to do some personal shopping as a follow-up to the image consulting. The hardest part was buying new shoes, because virtually nowhere stocks my size. But it was great and I got a few new ideas…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Monday I took the day off to do some personal shopping as a follow-up to the image consulting. The hardest part was buying new shoes, because virtually nowhere stocks my size. But it was great and I got a few new ideas.
  • Yeah so anyway the shoes have already chewed through one of my heels. Let’s try this again in a few weeks.
  • We’re in evaluation season, and as well as doing my own, for the first time in a long time it’s my turn to hear other people’s evaluations and later to calibrate them. This is interesting because — I realise that — since arriving in Japan I have been guessing what evaluators want to hear based on my experience in the UK & BG, and I wish I had known the differences a year ago.
  • Met up with ROL, in town on holiday, for some okonomiyaki and a natter. We walk around Shinjuku for a bit before noticing a place off to the side of a road and saying, “Huh, that looks interesting”. My first time doing the okonomiyaki dance by hand, and gosh, it’s both more fun and much…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Turn The Page — Week 20, 2026
item.description: Lots of architecture and design work this week. In the run-up to the end of the quarter there’s also a lot of small things to wrap-up but not enough time. Access & Identity (aka Auth) is a fascinating domain. I was…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Lots of architecture and design work this week. In the run-up to the end of the quarter there’s also a lot of small things to wrap-up but not enough time.
  • Access & Identity (aka Auth) is a fascinating domain.
  • I was summoned for some belated birthday drinks at The Aldgate which is a delightful pub. There I received some confirmation bias, and an interesting fortune reading. We tried to decode a curious social dynamic, met someone from my hometown and by the end of the night I had been thoroughly defeated in chess.
  • You might’ve noticed some design tweaks on this blog: Well, channelling enthusiasm I have made some long-overdue improvements including finishing setting up Storybook so I can do more design work in isolation, and fixing gripes with Gatsby + porting it to TypeScript.
  • The Yotsu Crew met up for some sake, some dinner and finished by taking the train for cocktails at Coto Academy. Mine was absinthe which was bold for a school night.
  • Unfortunately an event I was looking forward…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Brothers In Arms — Week 19, 2026
item.description: This week was golden week, coinciding with my birthday. Instead of a string of bank holidays and the string of ambitious (offline) travel plans, I was summoned and spent the week indoors working and ordering in the…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• This week was golden week, coinciding with my birthday. Instead of a string of bank holidays and the string of ambitious (offline) travel plans, I was summoned and spent the week indoors working and ordering in the entire inventory of CHOPPED SALAD DAY.
  • The work thing was a bit stressful, but it played to my strengths and I enjoyed supporting/rallying teams to accomplish a huge target, and as a little treat, I built (and evangelized) the codemod engine that I’ve wanted for most of my career.
  • On my birthday, D rallied some of my favourite people for a surprise dinner which ~ was a maid cafe. Genuinely masterful deception: Had I suspected anything, I would have not shown up. That’s a second bucket-list item crossed off this week, this one never to be repeated.
  • Followed by drinks in Arakicho, including at the insect bar. I was too wired to really be present or to really enjoy myself that evening but I am genuinely grateful.
  • The only plan I didn’t cancel all week was the Claude Monet exhibition at Artizon on Friday night which had been on my list since last year. This is my first time seeing his work in concentration and I really enjoyed his use of light. Followed by bar-hopping…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: The Shortest Straw — Week 18, 2026
item.description: This week is, well, busy. And the weather is genuinely perfect. At the start of the week I wrote a 30 minute talk, but postponed it at the eleventh hour after being delayed (and, a small case of nerves). Pretty happy…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• This week is, well, busy. And the weather is genuinely perfect.
  • At the start of the week I wrote a 30 minute talk, but postponed it at the eleventh hour after being delayed (and, a small case of nerves). Pretty happy with the state I got it into though.
  • Only went and injured myself running again, didn’t I!
  • Wednesday was a public holiday, and I spent it mostly catching up on errands.
  • The remainder of the week was spent speaking to many many teams and arranging boxes on a diagram, and then wrapping it up with a bow for a report.
  • Originally I had some ambitious weekend plans involving Kraftwerk and a beach party, but then something newsworthy happened and I found myself in Tamachi eating late-night curry and blasting music instead.
  • Clown Core are very good BTW.
  • Wrapped the week with a last-minute omakase okonomiyaki dinner in Jiyugaoka, and some karaoke in what can only be described as the backrooms.
  • Short notes on account of the weekend thing, innit.
  • All that awaits you is a somber…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: The Chase Is Better Than The Catch — Week 17, 2026
item.description: This week a new section was born in my Slack. That’s what kind of week it was. Really enjoying project work. All the frameworks are now in place. Architecture is mostly aligned. Real progress is happening. It has…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• This week a new section was born in my Slack. That’s what kind of week it was.
  • Really enjoying project work. All the frameworks are now in place. Architecture is mostly aligned. Real progress is happening.
  • It has come at the cost of a few late nights: One of which we had Fish & Chips, for me for the first time since moving to Tokyo. It was alright.
  • I enjoyed Wednesday.
  • Capped the week at Manager Camp, where for my self intro I talked about spice, symmetry and love. This was my first away-day since leaving Customer Products: We made a few big decisions and I had a great time working on problems with people I don’t normally get a chance to.
  • Dinner was nice: Rodizio!
  • Saturday marked the Nihonbashi sake tour, and I joined a group galavanting around. My favourite was actually the very first one I tried, and unfortunately I didn’t get its name. Capped the night at a bar playing classical chess, where I was totally blindsided and despite having the edge for most of the game, I lost…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Room Of Mirrors — Week 16, 2026
item.description: Between the weather and a project: Things are heating up this week. WithGreen’s taco-rice salad is pretty good. Lots of meetings in Japanese this week as we seek to resolve some important things. I enjoy it: It is…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Between the weather and a project: Things are heating up this week.
  • WithGreen’s taco-rice salad is pretty good.
  • Lots of meetings in Japanese this week as we seek to resolve some important things. I enjoy it: It is exactly the immersion I sought when coming to Japan.
  • Friday night I went to a club looking for someone whose phone had died, and spent the entire night looking for them. We didn’t meet, but I did encounter a verrrry popular influencer/streamer, who is much, much shorter IRL than Instagram makes it seem but otherwise exactly the same.
  • Nobody teaches you how to dress. At the weekend I took a big step and did a few rounds of image consulting and learned how wrong my wardrobe is: I’m a Bright Summer.
  • After catching up with CR, I do my first long run since last year. Traffic-lights continue to be frustrating.
  • Sunday was the first BBQ of the season, on a rooftop in Shibuya, followed by an after-party in the park. Tinnies in the park continue being peak.
  • Afterwards, equipped…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: No Leaf Clover — Week 15, 2026
item.description: A combination of shifting emotions and shifting priorities made this week a roller-coaster. Worked a lot on architecture this week. Both processing the results of last week’s workshop, and something new which is in the…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• A combination of shifting emotions and shifting priorities made this week a roller-coaster.
  • Worked a lot on architecture this week. Both processing the results of last week’s workshop, and something new which is in the pipeline and has an exciting deadline.
  • Did I mention I’m a manager now?
  • Restarted running: My progress is slow and painful but I am enjoying it.
  • Obanzai in Ebisu was delightful. Seriously underrated style of cuisine.
  • Taco Wednesday was a fun time. A vibe-coded game came up and we have been playing it all week.
  • On Friday the weather was awful, but I hung up my Martinitsa on a friendly tree and reaffirmed my wish.
  • We sponsor the Yokohama F. Marinos and Saturday was our sponsor match-day. The organising team adopted me for the day, which made the day incredibly memorable. After the match was over one of the mascots taught me how to pose.
  • On Sunday I forgot to buy tickets, but the weather was perfect, so after chilling in Nihonbashi for a few hours a pivot to biryani…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Sweet Amber — Week 14, 2026
item.description: A few things I was looking forward to happened this week, and the weather could not be better. Got some brutal feedback from a friend at a yakitori restaurant. The kind of brutal honesty you can only get from friends…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• A few things I was looking forward to happened this week, and the weather could not be better.
  • Got some brutal feedback from a friend at a yakitori restaurant. The kind of brutal honesty you can only get from friends, over chicken that is so fresh it allegedly expires after 30 seconds. Appreciated, thank-you.
  • Led an all-day architecture workshop in our Nagoya branch office. The purpose was to design and test a single API for a group of complex subsystems, which we did by modelling its entire domain and the journeys between them. We ran exactly to time, and answered the question we set out with.
  • Dinner was at the TikTok Izakaya, which it turns out, is in Nagoya. You know the one. It was a good time, and the food was alright, but there’s something unnerving about filming and live-streaming hapening one thin paper wall away which stifles conversation. “おフードのラストオーダー” made the night for me.
  • Very good long lunch with K before heading back to Tokyo for a meeting: Hitsumabushi. Maybe…

Read the full post here...
